title: AST DUI: Fairbanks [AK25028829]
summary: On March 27, 2025 at approximately 7:00pm, Alaska State Troopers in Fairbanks conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle at Nordale Road and Peede Road. The driver was identified as Travis Robert Wadsworth of North Pole. Investigation determined that Mr. Wadsworth was operating a motor vehicle while impaired by alcohol. He was arrested and later remanded to the Fairbanks Correctional Center.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Felony DUI: Wasilla [AK25028721]
summary: On 03/28/25 at 1446 hours, the Alaska State Troopers responded to the area of Meadow Lakes for the report of a REDDI where the driver was believed to be intoxicated. The vehicle was located in Big Lake, and the driver, Robert Wilson, age 65, of Big Lake, was arrested for felony DUI. He was transported to Mat-Su Pre-Trial without incident and held without bail pending arraignment.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST DV Criminal Mischief: Wasilla [AK25028555]
summary: On 03/28/2025, the Alaska State Troopers responded to the Williwaw subdivision for the report of a criminal mischief. Investigation revealed that Austin Cedillo, age 26, of Wasilla, had damaged property belonging to someone with whom he had a domestic relationship. AST did not locate Austin, and as a result, a felony warrant was requested for his arrest.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Arctic Sea Ice Extent: March 27, 2025
summary: March 27, 2025: The Arctic sea ice has experienced a decrease for this week at a rate of 2.13% (as opposed to last week’s increase rate of 0.98%), from the level on 03/20/2025 of 14.333 M km 2 to 14.028 M km 2 for this week. title: AST Fail to Register as a Sexual Offender: Fairbanks [AK25028687]
incident_type: Fail to Register as a Sexual Offender
summary: On 28 March 2025, at approximately 1146 hours, 911 received a report of the location of William Chickalusion (36M). A records check showed that William had failed to register as a sexual offender. Additionally, William had been convicted of failing to register as a sexual offender in 2024. William was located by Alaska State Troopers at Fairbanks Memorial Hospital, arrested, and remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center for failing to register in the first degree.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Fail to Register as a Sexual Offender

entities.address_detail: William had failed to register as a sexual offender

title: AST Warrant Arrest: Fairbanks [AK25025201]
summary: On 28 March 2025, at approximately 1051 hours, Alaska State Troopers contacted Gilbert Kanayurak (36M) at the Farmer's Loop West Transfer Site. A routine records check confirmed that Gilbert had an outstanding warrant for his arrest. Gilbert was arrested and remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center without any further issue.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: Gilbert had an outstanding warrant for his arrest

title: AST Failure to register SOR Status: Fairbanks [AK25028314]
summary: On 3/27/25, at 1600 hours, Robert Mayo age 65 of Fairbanks. was charged with one count of Failure to Register in the First Degree. Mayo is currently out of compliance with his SOR status. An arrest warrant has been requested in this matter.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Homicide Investigation: Sutton [AK25028413]
summary: On March 27, 2025, at around 8:25 pm, the Alaska State Troopers responded to the Sutton area to conduct a welfare check. Responding Troopers located 56-year-old Sutton resident Nicholas Wells, deceased from multiple gunshot wounds. Additional Troopers from the Alaska Bureau of Investigation and patrol responded to the area and identified 21-year-old Sutton resident Grant Wells as a person of interest in Nicholas Wells’s death. At around 12:30 am on March 28th, Troopers located Grant Wells’ vehicle near mile 164 of the Glenn Highway. Troopers tracked Grant Wells in waist-deep snow to a small cabin. After making commands for Grant Wells to exit the cabin, he started a fire in the cabin, which consumed the entire structure. Wells exited the cabin, and Troopers utilized less-lethal tools to ar
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
